title: Warmly Spiced Algerian Green Pepper Tagine
description: A briny and cheesy Algerian dish featuring green peppers, perfect for a low glycemic meal, with a rich and spicy flavor profile reminiscent of a monsoon season dish.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large clay or ceramic tagine or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the green peppers and cook until they start to soften.
Add the ground cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Pour in the chicken broth and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the peppers are tender.
Stir in the crumbled feta cheese and cook until it is melted and the sauce is creamy.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro.
Traditionally, the tagine is cooked over a low heat for a long period of time, which allows the flavors to meld together and the meat to become tender.
If using a clay tagine, make sure to soak it in water for 30 minutes before cooking to prevent cracking.
